title: Evaluation of urinary porphyrin excretion in neonates born to mothers exposed to airborne hexachlorobenzene.
ehr_evidence: No mention of EHR or linked health databases; study conducted in a Porphyria Unit at a tertiary care facility.
summary: A cross-sectional study evaluating urinary porphyrin excretion and hexachlorobenzene (HCB) levels in maternal serum and fetal cord blood in neonates born in a village near a chlorinated solvent factory to assess prenatal exposure effects.
key_findings: The study aimed to detect possible adverse effects in urinary porphyrin excretion caused by prenatal exposure to HCB.
limitations: Manuscript text is truncated at the end
